# `gdd.init` — Output template for `.gxp-dev.yaml`

The exact shape of the `.gxp-dev.yaml` manifest `gdd.init` writes at the consumer project root. Authoritative contract: `docs/project-layout.md` (toolkit) → "The `.gxp-dev.yaml` manifest". Substitute every `{{…}}` with a confirmed value — **the manifest must be marker-free and valid YAML**.

---

## Canonical shape

```yaml
# .gxp-dev.yaml — consumer manifest (generated by /gdd.init on {{today}})

# Toolkit version constraint
gxp_dev_version: "{{gxp_dev_version}}"   # default ">=0.1.0,<1.0.0"

# Identity
project_id: "{{project_id}}"
project_name: "{{project_name}}"
language: {{language}}                       # en | es | de | …

# Mode
mode: {{mode}}                               # develop | validate | hybrid

# Lifecycle
lifecycle: {{lifecycle}}                     # v-model | agile | hybrid
gamp_category: {{gamp_category}}             # 1 | 3 | 4 | 5  (required if profile == pharma)
lifecycle_phase: {{lifecycle_phase}}        # concept | project | operation | retirement

# Profile (domain + rigor)
profile: {{profile}}                         # pharma | medical-device | finance | aerospace | nuclear | general
rigor_level: {{rigor_level}}                 # light | standard | strict | regulated

# ID scheme
id_scheme: {{id_scheme}}                      # canonical | custom
# custom_alias only present when id_scheme == custom:
# custom_alias:
#   URS: "{{alias_for_urs}}"
#   FS:  "{{alias_for_fs}}"

# Active presets
presets:
  part11_active: {{part11_active}}           # activates URS-EREC + URS-ESIG preset reqs
  annex11_active: {{annex11_active}}
  gdpr_active: {{gdpr_active}}               # activates DPIA template
  annex22_active: {{annex22_active}}         # activates AISC template

# Active templates (subset of the toolkit's canonical set — derived + confirmed)
templates_active:
{{- for each acronym in the confirmed set }}
  - {{ACRONYM}}
{{- end }}

# Outputs
outputs:
  specs: {{specs}}                            # always true
  code: {{code}}
  tests: {{tests}}
  compliance_bundle: {{compliance_bundle}}

# Hybrid breakdown (ONLY when mode == hybrid)
# hybrid_breakdown:
#   vendor_components:
#     - name: "{{vendor_component_name}}"
#       gamp_category: {{n}}
#       templates_active: [URS, CS, SUP-ASSESS, IQ, OQ, PQ, RTM, VR]
#   custom_components:
#     - name: "{{custom_component_name}}"
#       gamp_category: 5
#       templates_active: [URS, FS, DS, ADR, IQ, OQ, PQ, RTM, VR, RN]
```

---

## F4-B note (only when an unusual category × mode combo was kept)

If the user confirmed an unconventional `gamp_category` × `mode` combination, append a single comment line right under the `mode:` field so reviewers see it was deliberate:

```yaml
mode: develop   # NOTE (F4-B): Cat 4 + develop is unconventional; kept by user choice on {{today}} — custom extensions on a configured platform
```

---

## Rules for filling

- **No raw `{{…}}`** in the written file. Every placeholder resolved to a confirmed value.
- **No `[NEEDS CLARIFICATION: …]`** in the manifest — it is a contract, not a draft spec. Unknowns are asked, not deferred.
- **`gamp_category`** is omitted only when `profile != pharma`; required otherwise.
- **`custom_alias`** block present only when `id_scheme: custom`. Aliases map `<DOC-TYPE>` prefixes only — never the 22 categories (they are the traceability spine).
- **`hybrid_breakdown`** present only when `mode: hybrid`; evaluate the F4-B warning per component.
- **`templates_active`** lists only acronyms that have a real `templates/csv/<ACRONYM>.md`.
- Keep field order as above for diff-friendliness across projects.
